description:
Goal
Make `src/compiler.rs` the authoritative owner of compiler stage sequencing so frontend preparation, execution-graph preparation, native preflight, and engine handoff are driven from one place instead of being split across `lib.rs`, `modules::pipeline`, and `ir_codegen::driver`.

Signal
- `Compiler` exposes the production pipeline entrypoints used by CLI, REPL, and tests.
- `ir_codegen` no longer owns the top-level native preflight sequencing; it consumes prepared inputs from the compiler spine.
- The authoritative-plan contract remains intact: planning still happens before codegen, and codegen emits no new `fz.planner.planned` events.
- Targeted pipeline tests and the fixture matrix pass.

Strategy
1. Introduce Compiler-owned driver APIs for frontend and execution-graph preparation, then move shared callers onto them.
2. Extract the native preflight/materialization sequence out of the codegen driver into a Compiler-owned stage boundary.
3. Finish by routing CLI/REPL/test-runner/interpreter entrypoints through the new spine and updating the agent docs.

References
- `src/compiler.rs`
- `src/frontend/mod.rs`
- `src/modules/pipeline.rs`
- `src/ir_codegen/driver.rs`
- `.agent/docs/single-authoritative-plan.md`
- `.agent/docs/modules.md`

Plan
```mermaid
graph LR
  A[frontend and execution graph APIs] --> B[native preflight extracted from codegen]
  B --> C[all entrypoints moved to Compiler spine]
```
title: Epic: factor compiler spine into Compiler
